title: options trading 101

evidence: The first basic strategy is to buy a call option on call when the stock price goes up.

title: options trading 101

evidence: Buying a call option is the simplest and most direct strategy we can do.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Call options simply put it means you spend money to buy a right to buy stocks at a fixed price.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: For example, let's say I buy a June 20th expiration date Nvidia call options with an exercise price of 120.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: If we put it into the previous definition on June 20th, no matter where Nvidia's stock price goes, I have the right to buy Nvidia stock at $120 per share.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Since it is a right to buy, it must cost money.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: As of the close on March 28th, the market price of this option was $6.7.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: It should be noted that $6.7 corresponds to the right to buy one share, while one option contract bundles 100 shares.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: So if I buy this option, I have the right to buy 100 shares of stock.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: On June 20th, if the stock price is above $120, such as 140, then I can exercise my rights to buy Nvidia stock at $120 per share, which means I earn $20 per share.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: And because I have paid $6.7 per share option fee, so the real net profit is $13.3.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Since one option corresponds to 100 shares, the total gain is $1,330.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Have you noticed that since buying options has a cost, only when the stock price exceeds the exercise price plus the option fee can I make a profit?

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: If the stock price only reached 125 on June 20th, my per share earnings after exercising the option would be $5 minus the cost of 6.7 per share.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: I actually lost $1.7 per share, which means the total loss of the option is $170.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: But if the final price does not reach $120, your maximum loss is the cost of buying the option, which is $670.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Buying a call option is a way to amplify gains when you confirm that the stock is in an uptrend.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Buying a put option, long put.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Suppose I buy a June 20th Nvidia put options with an exercise price of 105.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: That is to say, on June 20th, no matter where Nvidia's stock price goes, I have the right to sell Nvidia stock at $105 per share.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: As of the close on March 28th, the market price of this option was $7.9.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: On June 20th, if the stock price is below $15, such as 95, then I can exercise my right to sell Nvidia stock at $105 per share, which is equivalent to making $10 per share.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Since I have already paid the option premium of $79 per share, the real net profit is $21 per share, and the total option profit is $210.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: And the break even point for a put option is the strike price minus the option premium, which is 105 - 7.9 equals 97.1.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: So, as long as Nvidia's stock price falls below 97.1, I will make a profit.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Basic strategy three, selling cover calls.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: The essence of selling cover calls is selling call options.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Selling one cover call applies to 100 shares of stock as collateral, providing someone else with an opportunity to buy your stock.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: you're selling a promise to sell shares at a fixed price in the future.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: If you believe the stock price will fluctuate or rise slowly in the current phase, then selling a cover call is definitely a good option.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: For example, if I own a 100 shares of Nvidia stock and I think it's unlikely that Nvidia will reach a price of $150 in the near future, then I can choose to sell a cover call expiring on June 20th.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: I have a call option with a strike price of $150.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: The option price is currently $1.19.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: If I choose to sell, my account will immediately receive the 119 option premium as my profit.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: If Nvidia's stock price doesn't reach 150 by June 20th, if the option expires and I can continue to hold my 100 shares, then if Nvidia's stock price exceeds 150, my 100 Nvidia shares will be sold at the exercise price of 150.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: In this transaction, while I earned the option premium, I also forfeited all the potential gains the stock could have made above $150.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: On March 19th, investment tycoon Dan Young Ping announced on his personal social media that he had bought a 100,000 shares of Nvidia stock at $92.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: This is closely related to the cover call we just discussed.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: While buying 100,000 shares of Nvidia at $11670, it simultaneously sold 1,000 cover calls.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Since the premium for a call option is $2,400, 1,000 call options would amount to $2.4 million, a simple calculation will show us where the 92 price came from.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: In summary, the advantage of selling a cover call is that regardless of whether the stock price rises or falls, I can earn a premium upfront, which in turn lowers my cost basis.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: The disadvantage is that if the stock price surges above the strike price, I will be forced to sell.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Selling shares at the strike price forfeits all gains above that price.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Some curious readers might ask, if I don't already own the shares, can I directly sell call options?

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Selling naked calls has a maximum loss limit.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: At that time, you believe that shortselling sentiment towards Tesla was high and its performance was poor.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: So, you judge that the stock price would not rebound in the short term.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: So on that day, without owning the underlying stock, you sold a call option with a strike price of 240 that expired on March 28th.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: The option buyer will exercise the option, and you'll need to first buy a 100 shares of Tesla stock at $260 each and then sell them at $240 each, resulting in a total loss of $2,000.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: However, if you had bought a 100 shares of Tesla stock at $220 each on March 10th and then sold the option, your final profit would have been the option premium plus the $2,000 profit from the price increase.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: This is why I recommend selling covered calls instead of naked calls.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Basic strategy four, selling a self-put put.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Selling a put means you're willing to buy the stock at the strike price, and for this commitment, you can collect a premium upfront.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: When the market is stable or experiencing minor fluctuations and you're willing to buy the stock at a price lower than its current price, then a MyO is a good option.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: For example, if Nvidia's current stock price is $110 and you only want to buy $100 worth of Nvidia stock, but you think that price is unlikely to fall further, then you can choose to sell, for example, a put option with a strike price of $100 on June 20th.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: On June 20th, if the stock price drops to say 95, then you must buy a 100 shares of Nvidia at a price of 100.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: It is worth noting that selling put options requires margin.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Make sure you have enough cash to buy the stock at the exercise price if the stock price falls below the exercise price.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: For example, with this option, you need a margin of 100 by 100, which is $10,000.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: However, if you want to trade your options before the expiration date, you need to have a basic understanding of option price fluctuations.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: I want to emphasize that the factors influencing option price volatility are truly complex.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Back when I was studying at MIT, I was completely baffled when Professor Martin talked about option pricing.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: But if you want to trade options, then you at least need to understand every point I'm about to make.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Option prices are like a cake divided into two parts.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Intrinsic value is the actual value of the option.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Simply put, it's how much money you could make if you exercise the option right now.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: For example, you might buy a call option on a stock with a strike price of $100.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Because you can buy something worth 110 yuan for 100 yuan and the price difference is your profit.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: The value option it represents may change in the market before expiration and no one knows if the stock price will suddenly surge.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Therefore, time value is like buying the hope that you will pay to gamble on a future opportunity to make money.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: The longer the time horizon, the more valuable this hope becomes.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: The shorter the time frame, the smaller the hope and the lower the value.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Options are like a countdown timer.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: The rate at which the time value of an option decays is called theta.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: If the option is at the money, that is the strike price is similar to the current stock price.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: The market will no longer be willing to pay a high price for hope.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: This phenomenon is called IV crash.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: Option prices will also plummet, especially the time value part because the hope and the chance to gamble is squashed by reality.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: At the end of this video, I want to remind everyone here that while options can bring huge profits, there are also huge risks.

title: options trading 101
episode_tickers: NVDA, CAR, TSLA, ETH
evidence: If you will be affected by huge floating losses and it makes you lose sleep over it, then please think twice before buying options.
